mode_selection_reason: Pre-flight Operator-Directive Discovery in FUSE returned DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 as `status=pending`, but cross-check against the git clone (commit 3200e5b, +13min before this run) shows the directive is already `status=completed` (completed_by_run=tinker-2026-05-25T17-19, prop_id_authored=PROP-060). The FUSE workspace simply hasn't been synced yet — last workspace-sync ran at 17:07Z, before the 17:19 tinker commit. The next workspace-sync at ~18:00Z will pick up the completion. Conclusion: no directive work is actually pending; the FUSE-side `pending` flag is a transient sync artifact. Fall through to default Mode 3 (cost engineering + CLAUDE.md audit + context-load measurement) which the prior 17:19 run explicitly deferred 'to next Mode 3 run'.

preflight.directive_picked: none — DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 already completed in git; FUSE stale (next workspace-sync ~18:00Z resolves)

fuse_staleness_observation.issue: Tinker dispatcher reads operator-directives from the FUSE workspace (per tinker.md Pre-flight: Operator Directive Discovery — the node script uses relative path 'monitor/tinker/operator-directives/' resolved from cwd, which is FUSE for a default-invoked tinker run). FUSE is updated by hourly workspace-sync. When two tinker runs fire within the workspace-sync window — like 17:19 and 17:37 today, only 18 minutes apart — the second run sees a stale 'pending' status on a directive the first run just completed.
fuse_staleness_observation.today_observation: DIRECTIVE-20260525-003 in FUSE shows status=pending; in git it shows status=completed (commit 3200e5b at 17:19Z). PROP-060 file present in git but not in FUSE.
fuse_staleness_observation.scope_of_concern: Low. Dispatcher idempotency handles this: a re-pick of a now-completed directive would re-do the work, which is wasteful but not harmful (PROP file already exists — overwrite would be the same content; status flip is idempotent per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception). Real-world cost: a duplicate Opus run on the same directive content. Mitigated this run by manual git cross-check.
fuse_staleness_observation.could_be_PROP_material: Yes, marginally. A tinker pre-flight that cross-checks directive status against git (or against a clone if one exists) before treating FUSE as authoritative would close this race. Cost-benefit: small. Tracked as a candidate; not raising a PROP this run.
fuse_staleness_observation.recommended_action: No-op for now. Re-evaluate if duplicate-directive-pickups recur >2 times in the next 30d.

findings.title: Tinker run 17:19 left /tmp/tinker-clone behind; cleanup discipline gap.

evidence.claude_md_rule_quoted: 'The four scheduled agents (decider, curmudgeon, analyst, workspace-sync) clone responsibly already — each rm -rf's its working clone at run end.' Tinker is NOT in that list of 4.
evidence.tinker_md_rule_quoted: tinker.md does NOT contain an explicit 'rm -rf clone at end-of-run' instruction. The standing /tmp cleanup empowerment covers stale operator clones >=24h old but is silent on tinker's own working clone.
evidence.observed_artifact: /tmp/tinker-clone, 182MB, mtime 2026-05-25T17:16:59Z (created by run tinker-2026-05-25T17-19; left behind)

findings.root_cause: Tinker's prompt + CLAUDE.md does not explicitly require tinker to rm -rf its working clone at end-of-run. The standing /tmp-cleanup empowerment kicks in at >=24h mtime, so this clone won't be cleaned for nearly a day under current rules.
findings.recommended_action: Add an explicit cleanup line to tinker.md's outro: 'At end-of-run, rm -rf any clone created this run (typical path: /tmp/tinker-clone, /tmp/edit-clone-tinker).' Self-fixable next Mode 4 run. DEFERRED THIS RUN — avoid stacking prompt edits while PROP-060 is pending operator review.
findings.self_fix_blocker: Carry-forward to Mode 4 once PROP-060 lands or is rejected.

findings.title: Stale 'Eight agents' reference in tinker.md line 54 (should be 'Thirteen agents (eleven enabled)' to match actual scheduled-task count and CLAUDE.md Monitoring Pipeline table).

evidence.tinker_md_line_54_quoted: 'You maintain the monitoring pipeline for the ECM critical review. Eight agents, prompts in monitor/prompts/, outputs in monitor/.'
evidence.actual_count: 13 scheduled tasks per list_scheduled_tasks (11 enabled: poller, analyst, analyst-baby, curmudgeon, curmudgeon-verify, decider, integrity, tinker, social, prune-integrity, workspace-sync; 2 disabled: sloppytoppy-score, sloppytoppy-rewrite)
evidence.claude_md_consistency: CLAUDE.md says 'Thirteen scheduled agents are configured (eleven enabled)' — consistent with actual; tinker.md is the stale outlier.
evidence.blast_radius: Cosmetic/orientation only. Tinker still discovers actual agent state via list_scheduled_tasks + filesystem grep.

findings.root_cause: tinker.md was authored when the pipeline had 8 agents and was never updated as agents were added (analyst-baby 2026-05-13, curmudgeon-verify 2026-05-16, sloppytoppy-score 2026-05-16, sloppytoppy-rewrite 2026-05-16, prune-integrity 2026-05-23 — 5 added).
findings.recommended_action: 1-line prompt edit: 'Eight agents' → 'Thirteen agents (eleven enabled)'. Self-fixable; defer to bundle with FND-01's tinker.md edits when PROP-060 lands. Or operator-direct one-liner.
findings.self_fix_blocker: Same as FND-01 — avoid stacking edits on tinker.md while PROP-060 is unreviewed.

findings.title: Decider total context load grew +777L (+22.8%) measured / +287L (+8%) git-attributable since 2026-05-25T06-55 baseline; PROP-058 + PROP-059 implementations the proximate cause.

attribution_from_git_log: monitor/prompts/decider.md +204L (PROP-058 + PROP-059 — A0b blocked-on-curmudgeon sweep + mandatory fixed-pending-verification with fingerprint requirement)
monitor/prompts/reference/decider-reporting.md +6L (closure ledger reporting field additions per PROP-055)
evidence.attribution_gap: Sum of git-log deltas = +287L. Measured total delta = +777L. The +490L gap is likely caused by the 2026-05-25T06-55 baseline excluding some reference files from its measurement set. Treat the +777L number as upper-bound; the git-log-attributable delta is +287L (~+8% of the 3403 baseline).
evidence.alert_tier_per_tinker_md_rule: Tinker Mode 3 threshold: 'Flag any Opus agent whose total grew >10% since last report.' Conservative reading: +22.8% measured fires this threshold. Liberal reading: +8% real growth (git-log-attributable) is below.

findings.scope_of_concern: PROP-058 + PROP-059 each landed today; the decider growth is acquisition-of-functionality, not bloat. Net new capabilities: (a) Step A0b sweeps zombie blocked-on-curmudgeon entries, (b) mandatory pending-verification on self-apply closures with fingerprint requirement, (c) integrity Mech-A-bypass audit, (d) closure-ledger append on self-apply close path. All four were operator-approved.
findings.recommended_action: Monitor next 7d. If total stays at 4180 with no further +10% growth in the next two Mode 3 runs, treat as new steady-state. If growth continues, identify candidate sections of decider-patches-and-selfapply.md (now 751 lines, largest single ref) for split into a sub-reference (e.g., a separate decider-pending-verification.md). Do NOT do this preemptively — wait for confirmed bloat trend.
findings.self_fix_blocker: Premature optimization. The +287L growth is justified-by-feature.
